title: Docker Compose
description: Learn to define multi-container applications with Docker Compose and manage their lifecycle.

tasks.prompt: Start all services defined in docker-compose.yml in detached mode.
tasks.explanation: compose up creates networks, volumes, and containers from the YAML file.

tasks.prompt: Stop and remove containers, networks, and default volumes for the compose project.
tasks.explanation: compose down tears down the application stack cleanly.

tasks.prompt: List containers for the current compose project.
tasks.explanation: compose ps shows service names, states, and published ports.

tasks.prompt: Follow aggregated logs from all compose services.
tasks.explanation: compose logs -f streams output from every service in the project.

tasks.prompt: Build or rebuild images for all compose services.
tasks.explanation: compose build uses service build contexts defined in the YAML.

tasks.prompt: Build images and start services in detached mode.
tasks.explanation: --build ensures images are rebuilt before containers start.

tasks.prompt: Restart only the 'api' service in the compose project.
tasks.explanation: Targeting a service name restarts just that component.

tasks.prompt: Open an interactive bash shell in the running 'api' service container.
tasks.hint: Use docker compose exec api bash.
tasks.explanation: compose exec runs commands in an already running service container.

tasks.prompt: Pull the latest images for all compose services.
tasks.explanation: compose pull updates service images from their registries.

tasks.prompt: Validate and render the final compose configuration.
tasks.explanation: compose config catches YAML errors and shows interpolated values.
